about.title: Technology, volunteering, and a strong product mindset

paragraphs.one: I'm Andrea Tombolato, a full-stack developer since 2016. At Medas Solutions I work on healthcare software projects with a Java and Node.js stack, building reliable solutions where performance, code quality, and operational continuity truly matter.
paragraphs.two: Alongside my main role I work on small freelance projects under the heyatom brand, contribute to Element Gaming as a board member, and support Civil Protection as public communication lead.
paragraphs.three: Away from the screen, I recharge through mountain hikes, travel, and gaming culture. These experiences shape my design approach too: clean, accessible, grounded, and always people-first.

highlights.experience: {years}+ years across Java, Node.js, Vue, and Nuxt
highlights.focus: Clean architecture, accessibility, and interfaces shaped around real use cases
highlights.community: Across non-profit work, volunteering, and projects designed to generate local impact

timeline.title: Professional journey & community
timeline.description: From 2016 to today: full-stack development in healthcare, non-profit community projects, and local volunteering.

skills.title: Technologies and work areas
skills.description: Main stack: Vue.js and Nuxt.js on the frontend, Node.js and Nest.js on the backend, Java and Grails for enterprise systems. I work across SSR architectures, REST APIs and microservices, with a consistent focus on performance, accessibility and code quality. I cover the full development lifecycle — from interface design to production deployment.

volunteering.title: Civil Protection
volunteering.p1: Since 2015 I have been a volunteer in the Civil Protection group of Settimo Milanese. In 2021 I became squad leader, managing the group's evening shifts. In 2022 I was elected as a board member, choosing to step back from operational duties to handle external communications, an area the group needed to revive.
volunteering.p2: I relaunched the group's Facebook and Instagram pages, designed and developed the official website procivsettimomi.it.
volunteering.p3: In February 2026 I concluded my journey in Settimo Milanese. I am currently in the process of joining the Milan Municipal Civil Protection Group.

seo.title: Volunteering
seo.description: Since 2015 in Civil Protection: website, social media and alert bots for the Settimo Milanese group. Now joining the Milan Municipal Group.
seo.ogDescription: Since 2015 in Civil Protection: tech projects for the local community.

hero.title: Years serving the community
hero.subtitle: I applied the same skills I use for my clients to solve real problems in emergency contexts.

volunteer.title: Joined as a volunteer
volunteer.description: Began my journey in the Civil Protection group of Settimo Milanese. Operational training, duty shifts, and participation in exercises.

squadLeader.title: Squad leader
squadLeader.description: Managed the group's evening shift schedule and coordinated on-duty teams.

councillor.title: Elected board member
councillor.description: Elected to the group's board of directors. Voluntarily stepped back from operational duties to focus on governance and communications.

communications.title: Communications lead
communications.description: Relaunched Facebook and Instagram pages, designed and built the official website, and created an automated alert bot system for real-time volunteer notifications.

milan.title: Milan Municipal Group
milan.description: Concluded my journey in Settimo Milanese in February 2026. Currently in the process of joining the Milan Municipal Civil Protection Group.

website.description: Designed and built from scratch the official website for the Settimo Milanese Civil Protection group. Goal: an institutional and informative online presence for local citizens.

social.description: Inactive pages brought back to life after years of silence. Content strategy for institutional and operational communication: never alarmist, always informative.

bot.description: Automated notification system for volunteers. 24/7 monitoring of weather and operational alerts on the territory. Zero manual word-of-mouth, immediate response.

reflection.title: What Civil Protection taught me
reflection.p1: Communicating clearly under pressure. Having the right information is not enough — you need to deliver it at the right moment, in the right format, to the right person. That holds in an emergency just as much as in a product meeting.
reflection.p2: Coordinating across different institutions — the Municipality, the Prefecture, the Region — each with its own procedures, pace and language. I learned that adapting how you communicate is not a courtesy: it is the only way to actually make things happen.
reflection.p3: Building tools that have to work when it really matters. A bot that fails to send a notification at 3am is not a bug to fix in the next sprint — it is a volunteer who leaves late. That kind of responsibility changes the way you write code.
reflection.p4: The difference between showing up and leaving something behind. When I joined, the group had no website, the social pages were dormant and volunteers were contacted by phone. When I left, there were tools that kept running without me. That is the only measure that counts.

today.title: The next chapter
today.text: In February 2026 I concluded my journey in Settimo Milanese after eleven years. I am currently in the process of joining the Milan Municipal Civil Protection Group: a larger reality, with more complex scenarios and the same motivation as always.
